-## Lessons Learned (CRITICAL - READ THESE)
+## LESSONS LEARNED (CRITICAL)
 1. Lead promo DOES NOT affect evaluation price - only first billing
 2. Ongoing promo (set_promotion) DOES affect evaluation price
 3. Competitor events permanently raise quality expectations
-4. S1 stopped converting after day ~74 (now 122 days ago)
-5. Tenure loyalty keeps existing subs but doesn't help new leads
-6. At T4, Plan A quality = (base+shared+group_bonus)*1.0 (big upgrade from T3*0.9)
-7. S1-A has very low usage (~6 units/day) → low compute cost
-8. When removing group promo, must replace with per-plan promos to protect other-plan subs
-9. The $500/day dev burn was unsustainable - T4 upgrade gives more quality per dollar
-10. PMI at 45.9 means macro contraction - harder to get new subs
+4. S1 stopped converting at T2 quality (0.2771) even at $8/mo
+5. Tenure loyalty keeps existing subs (up to +0.13 quality bonus)
+6. T4 gives 33% quality boost over T2 — critical for conversion
+7. S1-A has low usage (~6 units/sub/day) at T2 → higher at T4
+8. When removing group promo, must replace with per-plan or per-customer promos
+9. The $500/day dev burn was unsustainable — T4 upgrade is better per dollar
+10. PMI contraction makes growth harder
+11. At T2, ALL 8 S1 leads this week were LOST (0% conversion)
+12. 369 open issues = reputation damage, negative social posts, fewer leads
+13. Capacity T0 at $85/day is minimum — can't reduce further
+14. S2 requires q_min=0.521 — unreachable without R&D or massive dev spend
+15. Enterprise groups unreachable (q_min > 0.66)

-- Consider market research to find more groups (costs $25K each)
